title: GET a URL and echo a named response header
prompt: Read two lines from standard input: a URL and a header name (each line is newline-terminated; trailing whitespace on either line is trimmed). Issue an HTTP GET request to that URL with a 5000 ms transport timeout. On a transport-successful HTTP 200 response, locate the named header (case-insensitive) and write its value followed by a newline, e.g. `application/json\n`. On any failure (transport error, non-200 status, or missing header), write the literal string `error\n` to standard output. Do not write to standard error. Exit with status 0 in every case. The header name match is case-insensitive on the name; the value bytes are echoed exactly as they appear after the colon and a single space (HTTP value trimming is delegated to the platform helper).

output_schema.stdout: either `<header_value>\n` on a successful match or `error\n` on any failure

notes_input_convention: Zero reads URL from argv[1] and header name from argv[2] because Zero 0.1.2 has no exposed stdin capability; the values are interpreted identically to the two stdin lines other languages read (trailing whitespace trimmed). All other languages read two newline-terminated lines from stdin. The verifier spins up a local HTTP fixture server before each language run and tears it down after; the fixture serves `/headers` (returns 200 with several headers), `/json` (returns 200 with Content-Type only), `/empty` (returns 200 with no extra headers), and `/404` (returns 404). The verifier passes inputs via stdin for non-Zero refs and via argv for Zero.
